Threat: Attacker uses stolen session token to impersonate customer
Mitigations: Short-lived tokens; secure cookies; MFA for risky actions; token revocation

Threat: XSS steals tokens or changes checkout flow
Mitigations: Output encoding; CSP; React safe rendering; dependency scanning

Threat: Sensitive data is stored in localStorage and read by injected scripts
Mitigations: HttpOnly cookies; minimize browser storage; CSP

Threat: Cart price or product ID is manipulated to reduce order total
Mitigations: Calculate prices on server; validate product IDs; transaction checks
OWASP: A01 Broken Access Control; A05 Injection

Threat: CORS misconfiguration allows hostile sites to read API responses
Mitigations: Strict CORS allowlist; no wildcard credentials; browser security tests

Threat: Missing authentication lets anonymous users access account APIs
Mitigations: OIDC; deny-by-default middleware; route-level auth tests

Threat: User changes role claims or IDs to call admin endpoints
Mitigations: Server-side authorization; signed claims; policy-as-code tests

Threat: User denies checkout because no correlation IDs or immutable audit events exist
Mitigations: Structured audit logs; request IDs; append-only log retention
OWASP: A09 Security Logging and Alerting Failures

Threat: Large JSON bodies or repeated checkout calls exhaust API resources
Mitigations: Body limits; rate limits; circuit breakers; queue expensive work
OWASP: A02 Security Misconfiguration; A06 Insecure Design

Threat: SQL injection modifies orders or product records
Mitigations: Parameterized queries; least-privilege DB role; input validation

Threat: SQL injection or overbroad query exposes customer/order data
Mitigations: Parameterized queries; row-level access checks; query allowlists
OWASP: A05 Injection; A01 Broken Access Control

Threat: Unencrypted backups expose customer emails and order history
Mitigations: Encrypted backups; restricted restore permissions; retention policy

Threat: Slow queries or missing indexes exhaust DB connections
Mitigations: Connection pooling; query timeouts; indexes; load testing

Threat: Redis exposed to network leaks cart/session data
Mitigations: Private subnet; Redis AUTH/TLS; security groups; no public endpoint

Threat: Cache poisoning changes session or authorization state
Mitigations: Signed cache values; key namespaces; TTLs; private networking
OWASP: A08 Software and Data Integrity Failures

Threat: Unbounded keys or spam traffic consumes cache memory
Mitigations: Rate limits; maxmemory policy; quotas; key expiry

Threat: Public receipt bucket exposes customer data
Mitigations: Block public access; bucket policies; private VPC endpoint; access analyzer

Threat: Attacker overwrites receipt objects or injects malicious content
Mitigations: S3 object versioning; KMS; IAM least privilege; content-type validation
OWASP: A08 Software or Data Integrity Failures

Threat: Receipt objects are deleted without trace
Mitigations: Object lock; versioning; CloudTrail data events; lifecycle approvals
OWASP: A09 Security Logging and Alerting Failures

Threat: Forged payment webhook marks unpaid orders as paid
Mitigations: Webhook signatures; timestamp window; nonce replay cache
OWASP: A07 Authentication Failures; A08 Software or Data Integrity Failures

Threat: Payment amount is changed between checkout and authorization
Mitigations: Server-side amount calculation; idempotency keys; compare provider response
OWASP: A06 Insecure Design; A08 Software or Data Integrity Failures

Threat: Payment dispute cannot be investigated because events are not logged
Mitigations: Persist signed payment events; correlation IDs; SIEM alerts
OWASP: A09 Security Logging and Alerting Failures

Threat: Phished admin credentials are used to change products or refunds
Mitigations: Admin MFA; device posture; privileged access management

Threat: Support user accesses owner-only admin functions
Mitigations: RBAC/ABAC; separation of duties; authorization tests

Threat: Compromised dependency or container image is deployed
Mitigations: SBOM; lockfiles; image signing; protected branches; scans

Threat: Build logs or image layers expose cloud credentials
Mitigations: Secret manager; masked logs; no secrets in Docker layers; rotation
OWASP: A04 Cryptographic Failures; A08 Software or Data Integrity Failures

Threat: Bot traffic or scraping overwhelms edge and origin
Mitigations: WAF rules; bot detection; per-IP/user limits; CDN caching

Threat: Cache key mistake serves one customer response to another
Mitigations: Do not cache authenticated API responses; cache-control tests

Threat: Stack traces reveal SQL structure secrets or internal hosts
Mitigations: Generic errors; centralized exception mapping; secret redaction
OWASP: A10 Server-Side Request Forgery and Error Handling Risks

Threat: Default credentials are reused in production
Mitigations: Environment separation; secret scanning; deployment policy checks

Threat: Overpermissive IAM role allows API to delete buckets or databases
Mitigations: Least privilege IAM; permission boundaries; access reviews
OWASP: A01 Broken Access Control; A02 Security Misconfiguration

Threat: Logs contain emails tokens or payment metadata
Mitigations: Log redaction; data classification; least-privilege log access
OWASP: A04 Cryptographic Failures; A09 Security Logging and Alerting Failures

Threat: Admin product/refund changes lack immutable audit trail
Mitigations: Admin audit events; tamper-resistant storage; alert on privileged actions
OWASP: A09 Security Logging and Alerting Failures

Threat: Container breakout or exposed Docker socket compromises host
Mitigations: No Docker socket mounts; rootless containers; runtime policies

Threat: Message body is modified before processing
Mitigations: Message signatures; schema validation; producer identity
OWASP: A08 Software and Data Integrity Failures

Threat: Message flood blocks consumers and delays critical work
Mitigations: Consumer autoscaling; quotas; backpressure; dead-letter queues
